verdict: 6 findings, 3 are P0 (fix this week). Performance is not the constraint; the funnel is.

findings.what: The primary CTA does not work
findings.why: A broken main action means every click intending to convert is lost. This is pipeline leaking in real time.
findings.fix: Fix the CTA target/handler so it routes to the intended booking or sign-up flow.
findings.measure: Track clicks vs completions on the CTA; target near-zero drop between click and next step.

findings.what: The strongest low-friction asset is hidden from the homepage
findings.why: A free trial / instant demo converts far better than a 'contact us' form, but only if visitors can find it.
findings.fix: Surface the self-serve asset as a primary homepage CTA alongside the sales CTA.
findings.measure: Track % of conversions coming through the self-serve path; target 30%+.

findings.what: The form cannot be routed or scored (no company field)
findings.why: Without company/role, marketing cannot score or route the lead and sales walks in blind.
findings.fix: Add a company field (and enrich role/size via Clay/Apollo on submit).
findings.measure: % of new leads with a resolvable company; target 95%+.

findings.what: Hero does not say what this is or who it is for
findings.why: If a buyer cannot place the product in 5 seconds, they leave before reaching the CTA.
findings.fix: Rewrite the hero as an outcome for a named buyer, e.g. 'Ramp reps in 15 days, not 90 — for enterprise sales teams.'
findings.measure: 5-second test with 5 target-persona users; 4/5 should describe the product correctly.

findings.what: 2 competing primary CTAs on the first screen
findings.why: More than one primary action splits attention and lowers the rate on all of them.
findings.fix: Pick one primary action; demote the rest to secondary styling.
findings.measure: A/B test single vs multiple primary CTA; measure primary-action click rate.

findings.why: Every extra required field measurably lowers completion.
findings.fix: Cut to name, work email, and one qualifier. Enrich the rest automatically after submit.
findings.measure: Measure completion rate before/after; expect an 8-15% lift from trimming alone.
